cards.name: Drinking Trough
cards.text: Each of your pastures (with or without a stable) can hold up to 2 more animals.  [CLARIFICATION: Cards holding animals are not pastures unless explicitly stated.]
cards.tag_note: Continuous +2 capacity to each existing pasture.

cards.name: Young Animal Market
cards.text: You immediately get 1 cattle. (Effectively, you are exchanging 1 sheep for 1 cattle.)
cards.tag_note: The gained cattle is an animal needing farm accommodation → E-ANIMALS, not E-GOODS (goods/food/crops only); passing minor per the catalog flag.

cards.name: Game Trade
cards.text: You immediately get 1 wild boar and 1 cattle. (effectively, you are exchanging 2 sheep for 1 wild boar and 1 cattle.)
cards.tag_note: Passing minor (catalog passing_left='X') that grants animals on play; animals are E-ANIMALS not E-GOODS (labelA's E-GOODS is wrong), and the 2-sheep play cost is an ordinary animal cost needing no code.

cards.name: Animal Tamer
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get your choice of 1 wood or 1 grain. Instead of just 1 animal total, you can keep any 1 animal in each room of your house.  [CLARIFICATION: This effect is negated by the Milking Place D012.]
cards.tag_note: On-play wood-or-grain gain (ONPLAY + E-GOODS) plus a continuous per-room pet-capacity rule (PASSIVE + E-CAPGROW, which explicitly covers house-pet/per-room). F-MANDCHOICE is a HOOK dimension and may only accompany HOOK, so it is dropped; the on-play choice carries no code.

cards.name: Consultant
cards.text: When you play this card in a 1-/2-/3-/4- player game, you immediately get 2 grain/3 clay/2 reed/2 sheep.  [CLARIFICATION: In a 5- or 6-player game, you receive 2 sheep.]
cards.tag_note: On-play gain of goods (grain/clay/reed) or sheep depending on player count; animals must be accommodated. Not NONE since animals require accommodation.

cards.name: Rammed Clay
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 clay. You can use clay instead of wood to build fences.  [CLARIFICATION: You can use both wood and clay for the same “Build Fences” action.]
cards.tag_note: On play +1 clay; continuous rule allowing clay to substitute for wood on fences.

cards.name: Forest School
cards.text: You can consider the "Lessons" action spaces not occupied. You can replace each food that an occupation costs with wood.
cards.tag_note: Two always-on rules: treat Lessons as unoccupied (L-OCCUPY, and 'considering a space unoccupied' is an explicit L-EXT example) and pay occupation food costs in wood (currency substitution — E-COSTMOD, also an explicit L-EXT example), so L-EXT belongs alongside both.

cards.name: Loppers
cards.text: Each time you build 1 or more fences, you can also use this card to exchange 1 wood and 1 fence in your supply for 2 food and 1 bonus point.
cards.tag_note: "Each time you build fences" = own fence sub-action hook, T-BEFORE default; the exchange spends a fence piece (E-PIECECOST) and converts goods (E-CONVERT — food output is part of the conversion, so no separate E-GOODS); bonus points accrue over the game (ST-COUNTER).

cards.name: Lumber Mill
cards.text: Every improvement costs you 1 wood less.
cards.tag_note: Every improvement costs 1 wood less; always-on cost reduction.

cards.name: Carpenter's Parlor
cards.text: Wooden rooms only cost you 2 wood and 2 reed each.
cards.tag_note: Continuous cost change: wooden rooms cost 2 wood + 2 reed.

cards.name: Mini Pasture
cards.text: Immediately fence a farmyard space, without paying wood for the fences. (If you already have pastures, the new one must be adjacent to an existing one.)
cards.tag_note: On-play fence of one space at no wood cost (E-GRANTSUB + E-FREEFENCE); the must-be-adjacent-to-an-existing-pasture constraint is genuine orthogonal farm-tile adjacency (L-GEOMFARM); nothing in the text passes the card, so E-PASSING is wrong.

cards.name: Forestry Studies
cards.text: Each time after you use the "Forest" accumulation space, you can return 2 wood to that space to play 1 occupation without paying an occupation costs.
cards.tag_note: Optional after-Forest hook granting an occupation play at no occupation cost; the taxonomy lists play-a-card under S-SUB primitives, so the grant is E-GRANTSUB (not E-GRANTACT), and 'without paying occupation costs' is E-COSTMOD. Returning 2 wood is an ordinary cost, not E-CONVERT (nothing is exchanged for goods at a rate).

cards.name: Chick Stable
cards.text: Add 3 and 4 to the current round and place 2 food on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: One-shot on play; all food goes onto future round spaces (E-SCHED only — no immediate goods, so labelA's E-GOODS is wrong); 1 Wood/1 Clay is a choice of play cost for the same effect (E-ALTCOST).

cards.name: Club House
cards.text: Place 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces and 1 stone on the round space after that. At the start of these rounds, you get the respective good.
cards.tag_note: One-shot scheduling of food+stone onto future round spaces; 3 Wood/2 Clay is an alternative play cost for the same effect (E-ALTCOST, missed by labelA).

cards.name: Straw-Thatched Roof
cards.text: You no longer need reed to renovate or build a room.
cards.tag_note: Continuously removes reed requirement from renovate/build-room costs.

cards.name: Field Fences
cards.text: You can immediately take a "Build Fences" action, during which you do not have to pay wood for fences that you build next to field tiles.
cards.tag_note: On play grants a full optional Build Fences action (E-GRANTACT) with a positional discount — fences adjacent to field tiles are free (E-FREEFENCE + L-GEOMFARM, tile adjacency); the 2-Food play cost gets E-FOODCOST per the cost rulings.

cards.name: Clay Supports
cards.text: Each time you build a clay room, you can pay 2 clay, 1 wood, and 1 reed instead of 5 clay and 2 reed.
cards.tag_note: An alternative build-cost formula for clay rooms is a continuous cost-rule change resolved inside payment enumeration, not a fired event — the taxonomy's PASSIVE definition explicitly covers cost changes; the 'you can... instead' choice is just picking among payment options.

cards.name: Wooden Whey Bucket
cards.text: Each time before you use the "Sheep Market"/"Cattle Market" accumulation space, you can build exactly 1 stable for 1 wood/at no cost.
cards.tag_note: Before-space hook granting an optional stable build; the grant carries its own discounted cost (1 wood / free vs normal 2 wood) -> E-COSTMOD. E-PIECECOST is wrong (the stable is built, not paid with). Play cost includes 1 Food -> E-FOODCOST per the ruling.

cards.name: Roof Ladder
cards.text: Each time you renovate, you pay 1 fewer reed and, at the end of the action, you get 1 stone.
cards.tag_note: Renovate hook with two parts: the reed discount applies when paying (T-BEFORE, E-COSTMOD) and the stone comes explicitly 'at the end of the action' (T-AFTER, E-GOODS) — both timings are real, so labelB is right to tag both.

cards.name: Hunting Trophy
cards.text: Improvements built on "House Redevelopment" cost you 1 building resource of your choice less. Fences built on "Farm Redevelopment" cost you a total of 3 wood less.  [CLARIFICATION: Alone, “built” can refer to either improvement type.  《lmprovements played by Merchant C096 do not get discounted.》]
cards.tag_note: Passive discounts: −1 building resource on House-Redev improvements (E-COSTMOD) and −3 wood on Farm-Redev fences (E-FREEFENCE). The 'Return or Cook 1 Wild Boar' cost forks the play into cook-for-food (ONPLAY E-GOODS) vs plain return — a cost/reward fork = E-PLAYVARIANT. The card consumes a boar, never provides one, so labelA's E-ANIMALS is wrong.

cards.name: Briar Hedge
cards.text: You do not need to pay wood for fences that you build on the edge of your farmyard board.  [CLARIFICATION: You must still use your fence pieces.]
cards.tag_note: Free fences on the farmyard-board EDGE = farmyard geometry (L-GEOMFARM), confirmed.

cards.name: Ash Trees
cards.text: When you play this card, immediately place (up to) 5 fences from your supply on it. When you build fences, fences taken from this card cost you nothing.
cards.tag_note: The 5 fences are staged onto the card as a persistent pool and later built for free — they are not spent as a payment, so E-PIECECOST (a cost paid with a piece) does not apply; on-play stocking (ONPLAY+ST-STACK) plus an always-on free-when-taken-from-card rule (PASSIVE+E-FREEFENCE).

cards.name: Frame Builder
cards.text: Each time you build a room/renovate, but only once per room/action, you can replace exactly 2 clay or 2 stone with 1 wood.  [CLARIFICATION: The effect may be applied once for each room built.]
cards.tag_note: On build-room/renovate, once per room/action, replace 2 clay/stone with 1 wood = optional cost substitution.

cards.name: Hedge Keeper
cards.text: Each time you take a "Build Fences" action, you do not have to pay wood for 3 of the fences you build.  [CLARIFICATION: You may spend 0 wood to build 1-3 fences.  Mini Pasture B002, Overhaul C001, and other fencing effects that are not the literal “Build Fences” action, do not trigger this card.]
cards.tag_note: A standing cost discount applied automatically whenever the literal Build Fences action is taken — the same shape as the PASSIVE exemplar 'every improvement costs 1 wood less'; nothing fires and no separate choice is offered (the free budget just applies to fences you build), so no HOOK/F-TRIG. E-FREEFENCE for the 3 free fences.

cards.name: Carpenter
cards.text: Every new room only costs you 3 of the appropriate building resource and 2 reed (e.g. if you live in a wooden house, 3 wood and 2 reed).
cards.tag_note: Rooms cost 3 building resource + 2 reed — passive cost change.

cards.name: Master Bricklayer
cards.text: Each time you build a major improvement, reduce the stone cost by the number of rooms you have built onto you initial house.  [CLARIFICATION: If you have 3 rooms total, the discount is 1 stone.]
cards.tag_note: An always-on cost-reduction rule computed from current state (rooms built beyond the initial 2) — exactly the taxonomy's PASSIVE example ('every improvement costs 1 wood less') + E-COSTMOD. No firing event and nothing to store (the room count is live state), so labelA's HOOK/S-SUB/ST-STORE are wrong.

cards.name: Bricklayer
cards.text: Each improvement and each renovation cost you 1 clay less. Each room costs you 2 clay less.
cards.tag_note: Always-on cost reduction on improvements/renovations/rooms.

cards.name: Wooden Hut Extender
cards.text: Wood rooms now cost you 1 reed, and additionally 5 wood through round 5, 4 wood in rounds 6 and 7, and 3 wood in round 8 and later.
cards.tag_note: Passive continuous cost-formula change for wood rooms; round-dependent cost is computable from round_number, no per-card stored state.

cards.name: Wood Expert
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 2 wood. Each improvement costs you up to 2 wood less, if you pay 1 food instead.
cards.tag_note: ONPLAY +2 wood (E-GOODS); passive cost-modifier substituting up to 2 food for 2 wood on each improvement (E-COSTMOD); the substitution is paid in food -> E-FOODCOST.

cards.name: Clay Plasterer
cards.text: Renovating to clay only costs you exactly 1 clay and 1 reed. Each clay room only costs you 3 clay and 2 reed to build.
cards.tag_note: Continuous cost replacement: renovate-to-clay costs 1 clay+1 reed; each clay room costs 3 clay+2 reed.

cards.name: Millwright
cards.text: You immediately get 1 grain. Each time you build fences, stables, and rooms, or renovate your house, you can replace up to 2 building resources of any type with 1 grain each.  [CLARIFICATION: Allows 1 or 2 substitutions per type of thing built.  Common misread is to forget the last “each”.]
cards.tag_note: On-play +1 grain, plus a passive pay-time substitution (grain in place of building resources when building/renovating) — that is E-COSTMOD's 'substituting one resource for another when paying', not a standalone at-will exchange, so E-CONVERT (labelA) is wrong.

cards.name: Stablehand
cards.text: Each time you build at least 1 fence, you can also build a stable without paying wood for the stable.
cards.tag_note: Fires on your fence-build sub-action ('also' = after), granting an optional stable build whose printed 1-wood cost is waived — a cheaper build, so E-COSTMOD applies (consistent with D95 tagging the granted build's cost change).

cards.name: Beer Keg
cards.text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can use this card to exchange 1/2/3 grain for 0/1/2 bonus points and exactly 3 food.
cards.tag_note: Optional exchange DURING the feeding phase — the 3 food must be available to pay feeding, so it fires before the feeding resolves (T-BEFORE); grain→food+bonus-points is a conversion producing goods, and points earned through an action legitimately combine HOOK+E-SCORE. A-OWN is mandatory.

cards.name: Loom
cards.text: In the field phase of each harvest, if you have at least 1/4/7 sheep, you get 1/2/3 food. During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for every 3 sheep.
cards.tag_note: Field phase of each harvest: sheep-threshold food; plus end-game 1 pt per 3 sheep (E-SCORE).

cards.name: Butter Churn
cards.text: In the field phase of each harvest, you get 1 food for every 3 sheep and 1 food for every 2 cattle you have.
cards.tag_note: Field phase of each harvest: food per 3 sheep and per 2 cattle.

cards.name: Crack Weeder
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food. For each vegetable you take from a field in the field phase of a harvest, you also get 1 food.
cards.tag_note: On play +1 food; +1 food per vegetable taken from a field in the field phase.

cards.name: Three-Field Rotation
cards.text: At the start of the field phase of each harvest, if you have at least 1 grain field, 1 vegetable field, and 1 empty field, you get 3 food.
cards.tag_note: Recurring harvest-field-phase hook ('at the start of the field phase' = T-BEFORE); the card has no once-when-played effect, so ONPLAY is wrong — HOOK is the sole activation; field-composition condition is a plain state read.

cards.name: Elephantgrass Plant
cards.text: Immediately after each harvest, you can use this card to exchange exactly 1 reed for 1 bonus point.
cards.tag_note: 'Immediately after each harvest' = after the whole harvest, i.e. after the breeding phase (T-AFTER+S-HBREED), not S-AFTERFEED which is the distinct mid-harvest moment between feed and breed. Optional (F-TRIG). Reed->bonus-point is not good-for-good, so no E-CONVERT (reed is an ordinary cost); earned bonus points need a running tally (ST-COUNTER).

cards.name: Studio
cards.text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can use this card to turn exactly 1 wood/clay/stone into 2/2/3 food.
cards.tag_note: Optional conversion available during the feeding phase (S-HFEED), usable before the feeding payment resolves -> T-BEFORE (labelA omitted the required timing dimension). Wood/clay/stone -> food at a rate = E-CONVERT + E-GOODS.

cards.name: Schnapps Distillery
cards.text: In each feeding phase, you can use this card to turn exactly 1 vegetable into 5 food. During scoring, you get 1 bonus point each for your 5th and 6th vegetable.
cards.tag_note: Optional per-feeding-phase conversion (HOOK/S-HFEED/F-TRIG/A-OWN/E-CONVERT) — all four HOOK dimensions are mandatory, so a timing tag is required and T-BEFORE fits (the conversion is available before the feeding payment resolves; labelA omitted timing). The 5th/6th-vegetable bonus is a plain E-SCORE rule.

cards.name: Milking Stool
cards.text: In the field phase of each harvest, if you have at least 1/3/5 cattle, you get 1/2/3 food. During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for every 2 cattle you have.
cards.tag_note: Field phase of each harvest: get food by cattle threshold; plus end-game bonus per 2 cattle.

cards.name: Bale of Straw
cards.text: At the start of each harvest, if you have at least 3 grain fields (including field cards with planted grain), you get 2 food.
cards.tag_note: At start of each harvest, if >=3 grain fields get 2 food.

cards.name: Beer Tap
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 2 food. In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can turn 2/3/4 grain into 3/6/9 food.
cards.tag_note: On play get 2 food; during feeding phase of each harvest optionally convert grain->food at a scaling rate.

cards.name: Lynchet
cards.text: In the field phase of each harvest, you get 1 food for each harvested field tile that is orthogonally adjacent to your house.
cards.tag_note: Which field tiles were harvested is not recoverable from post-phase state (an emptied field looks never-sown), so the field-phase event payload is needed → ST-PROV; adjacency-to-house is L-GEOMFARM.

cards.name: Grain Sieve
cards.text: In the field phase of each harvest, if you harvest at least 2 grain, you get 1 additional grain from the general supply.
cards.tag_note: "Harvest at least 2 grain" reads the amount yielded this field phase, which merges into supply and is not reconstructible afterward — needs the harvest event payload → ST-PROV.

cards.name: Stable Manure
cards.text: In the field phase of each harvest, you can harvest 1 additional good from a number of fields equal to the number of unfenced stables you have.
cards.tag_note: Field phase: may harvest 1 extra good from fields equal to unfenced stables; changes field yield rule.

cards.name: Wood Harvester
cards.text: In the field phase of each harvest, you get 1 wood/1 food for each wood accumulation space with exactly 2 wood/at least 3 wood.
cards.tag_note: Mandatory goods in each harvest field phase; T-AFTER is reserved for explicit 'immediately after / at the end of' language, which is absent, so the default T-BEFORE applies (timing is mechanically immaterial here anyway).

cards.name: Slurry Spreader
cards.text: In the field phase of each harvest, each time you take the last grain/vegetable from a field, you also get 2 food/1 food.
cards.tag_note: Fires in field phase when the last crop is taken from a field, giving food; keyed on field-harvest depletion event.

cards.name: Scythe Worker
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 grain. In the field phase of each harvest, you can harvest 1 additional grain from each of your grain fields.
cards.tag_note: On play +1 grain (ONPLAY+E-GOODS). The field-phase extra grain per grain field says 'you can' — optional, so F-TRIG, not F-AUTO (labelA's error). Changing how much a field yields in the field phase is E-CROPMANIP; own-harvest hook at the field-phase seam (S-HFIELD/T-AFTER).

cards.name: Potato Harvester
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 3 food. For each vegetable you get from your fields during the field phase of the harvest, you get 1 additional food.
cards.tag_note: On-play 3 food plus a per-harvest field-phase hook scaling with vegetables just yielded; after the field phase the yield count is no longer derivable from state (a field that held 1 veg is now indistinguishable from an empty one), so the event payload is needed → ST-PROV.

cards.name: Schnapps Distiller
cards.text: In the feeding phase of each harvest, you can use this card to turn exactly 1 vegetable into 5 food.
cards.tag_note: 'Use this card' is activation flavor, not a worker-placement action space, so no L-CARDSPACE; an optional feeding-phase conversion needs its HOOK timing dimension, and it is available before the feed payment resolves → T-BEFORE.

cards.name: Home Brewer
cards.text: After the field phase of each harvest, you can use this card to turn exactly 1 grain into your choice of 3 food or 1 bonus point.
cards.tag_note: Optional after-field-phase hook converting 1 grain to 3 food or 1 bonus point (E-CONVERT + E-SCORE); repeated bonus points are a global VP tally, not per-card state — precedent (end-of-harvest stone-house bonus point) carries no ST-COUNTER.

cards.name: Winter Caretaker
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 grain. At the end of each harvest, you can buy exactly 1 vegetable for 2 food.
cards.tag_note: On-play +1 grain; 'end of each harvest' consistently maps to S-AFTERFEED (not S-HBREED) per three precedents including this card's prior row; the 2-food veg buy is E-CONVERT + E-FOODCOST.

cards.name: Cube Cutter
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood. In the field phase of each harvest, you can use this card to exchange exactly 1 wood and 1 food for 1 bonus point.
cards.tag_note: On-play wood = ONPLAY+E-GOODS. Optional field-phase exchange = HOOK/S-HFIELD/F-TRIG/A-OWN; no 'after'/'end of' wording so T-BEFORE. The exchange costs 1 food → E-FOODCOST, and earns a bonus point through an action → E-SCORE (legit with HOOK). Accrued bonus points fold into scoring; a per-card counter is a plausible implementation but the card mandates no stored state beyond ordinary bookkeeping — I keep ST-COUNTER since points earned mid-game must be tallied across harvests.

cards.name: Shifting Cultivation
cards.text: Immediately plow 1 field.
cards.tag_note: On play, immediately plow 1 field (granted plow), then pass.

cards.name: Sleeping Corner
cards.text: You can use any "Wish for Children" action space even if it is occupied by one other player's person.  [CLARIFICATION: But not if occupied by 2+ other player’s people.]
cards.tag_note: Always-on continuous rule change letting you use an occupied Wish for Children space (PASSIVE + L-OCCUPY). No firing event, no goods.

cards.name: Debt Security
cards.text: During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each major improvement you have, up to the number of your unused farmyard spaces.
cards.tag_note: Pure end-game scoring rule (E-SCORE, no HOOK per ruling) — but >=1 ACTIVATION is required and a standing scoring rule is PASSIVE; the 2-food play cost gets E-FOODCOST per the cost ruling (food costs are flagged, unlike ordinary resources). Min(majors, unused spaces) is a plain count, not E-SCOREOPT.

cards.name: Pottery Yard
cards.text: During the scoring, if there are at least 2 orthogonally adjacent unused spaces in your farm, you get 2 bonus points. (You still get the negative points for those unused spaces.
cards.tag_note: Pure end-game scoring rule (computed, never hooked) keyed on orthogonal adjacency of unused farm spaces = E-SCORE + L-GEOMFARM; PASSIVE supplies the required activation for an always-on scoring rule. Threshold check, not an arrangement optimization, so no E-SCOREOPT.

cards.name: Mantlepiece
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 bonus point for each complete round left to play. You may no longer renovate your house.
cards.tag_note: Bonus points earned at play time from rounds remaining must be snapshotted until scoring (ST-STORE); 'you may no longer renovate' is a permanent always-on rule change (PASSIVE). L-EXT is for extending legality, not restricting it.

cards.name: Bottles
cards.text: For each person you have, you must pay an additional 1 clay and 1 food to play this card.  [CLARIFICATION: A newborn is a person.]
cards.tag_note: The play cost includes food (1 per person), which the rulings explicitly flag as E-FOODCOST (payable via conversions); the per-person clay is an ordinary resource cost needing no code. A real mechanic applies, so NONE is wrong.

cards.name: Wood Pile
cards.text: You immediately get a number of wood equal to the number of people you have on accumulation spaces.
cards.tag_note: One-shot wood gain scaled by people currently on accumulation spaces — needs worker-placement locations (ST-PLACELOG); the catalog marks it passing_left=X (Bubulcus traveler), so E-PASSING belongs (labelA missed it).

cards.name: Excursion to the Quarry
cards.text: You immediately get a number of stone equal to the number of people you have.  [CLARIFICATION: A newborn is a person.]
cards.tag_note: One-shot stone gain per person (newborns count — plain state read); the printed 2-Food play cost is exactly the food-that-may-need-conversion case the rulings say to flag (E-FOODCOST); passing traveler (passing_left=X).

cards.name: Stable
cards.text: Immediately build 1 stable. (The stable costs you nothing, but you must pay the cost shown on this card.)
cards.tag_note: On play grants a build-one-stable sub-action (free — E-GRANTSUB) and passes on; the play cost is 1 Wood, an ordinary resource, and the stable piece placed is the EFFECT not a cost, so E-PIECECOST is wrong.

cards.name: Christianity
cards.text: When you play this card, all other players get 1 food each.
cards.tag_note: On play, each other player gains 1 food (gift to opponents).

cards.name: Plant Fertilizer
cards.text: In each field with exactly 1 good, you can immediately place 1 additional good of the same type.  [CLARIFICATION: Boar held on unplanted fields (from Mud Patch A011) do not apply for this effect.  《If there is exactly 1 wood on the Wood Field D075, another wood may be added on either the same or a different stack.》]
cards.tag_note: Add 1 good to each field holding exactly 1 good; passing minor. Manipulates crops on fields.

cards.name: Dwelling Plan
cards.text: You can immediately take a "Renovation" action.
cards.tag_note: Grants the renovate primitive — explicitly listed under E-GRANTSUB (no standalone 'Renovation' action space exists), not a whole-action grant; passing minor; 1-Food play cost = E-FOODCOST per the cost ruling.

cards.name: Wood Rake
cards.text: During scoring, if you had at least 7 goods in your fields before the final harvest, you get 2 bonus points.
cards.tag_note: Scoring rule (E-SCORE, PASSIVE activation) that depends on the field-goods count BEFORE the final harvest — the engine must snapshot that value, hence ST-STORE.

cards.name: Summer House
cards.text: During scoring, if you live in a stone house, you get 2 bonus points for each unused farmyard space orthogonally adjacent to your house. (You still lose the points for these unused spaces.)
cards.tag_note: Scoring rule (E-SCORE, PASSIVE activation) keyed on unused spaces ORTHOGONALLY ADJACENT to the house — genuine farm-tile adjacency, so L-GEOMFARM.

cards.name: Field Clay
cards.text: You immediately get 1 clay for each planted field you have.
cards.tag_note: Immediate clay per planted field (plain state read, E-GOODS) on a passing minor; the 1-Food play cost is flagged E-FOODCOST because food costs may need crop/animal conversion (the explicit exception in the cost ruling).

cards.name: Petrified Wood
cards.text: Immediately exchange up to 3 wood for 1 stone each.
cards.tag_note: Pure on-play exchange (wood->stone) is E-CONVERT; the gained stone comes only via the conversion, so E-GOODS is redundant over-tagging. passing_left='X' in the data confirms E-PASSING.

cards.name: Conservator
cards.text: You can renovate your wooden house directly to stone without renovating it to clay first.  [CLARIFICATION: You may decline this ability.]
cards.tag_note: A continuous rule adding wood→stone as a renovation target — exactly L-EXT's 'a new renovation target'. It doesn't change what any renovation costs (you still pay stone + reed for the renovation you take), so no E-COSTMOD.

cards.name: Furniture Carpenter
cards.text: Each harvest, if any player (including you) owns the Joinery or an upgrade thereof, you can buy exactly 1 bonus point for 2 food.
cards.tag_note: 'Each harvest, you can buy' parallels the 'Once per round, you can' pattern - an at-will option capped per harvest (no firing seam; the buy floats within the harvest), costing 2 food for a bonus point; bought points accumulate over the game (ST-COUNTER). The Joinery condition is a plain state read.

cards.name: Roof Ballaster
cards.text: When you play this card, you can immediately pay 1 food to get 1 stone for each room you have.  [CLARIFICATION: The 1 food (total, not per room) is in addition to the occupation cost.]
cards.tag_note: One-shot optional on-play purchase: pay 1 food (E-FOODCOST) for stone per room (plain count read, E-GOODS); not E-CONVERT — that is for a standing rate-exchange ability, and the parallel pay-food-for-animals on-play cards carry no E-CONVERT.

cards.name: Tutor
cards.text: During scoring, you get 1 bonus point for each occupation played after this one.  [CLARIFICATION: This card only refers to cards played by the owner.]
cards.tag_note: Pure end-game scoring rule: per the ruling E-SCORE is computed at scoring and never carries HOOK/S-PLAY/F-AUTO; the play-order bookkeeping is just per-card state (ST-COUNTER), not a fired mechanic.

cards.name: Butler
cards.text: If you play this card in round 11 or before, during scoring, you get 4 bonus points if you then have more rooms than people.
cards.tag_note: Rooms-vs-people is a self-comparison, not a both-players comparison — E-SCORECMP is wrong. The played-by-round-11 condition must be snapshotted at play time (ST-STORE); the scoring rule itself is a passive computed bonus (PASSIVE + E-SCORE, no HOOK).

cards.name: Second Spouse
cards.text: You can use the "Urgent Wish for Children" action space (from round 12-13) even if it is occupied by the first person another player placed.  [CLARIFICATION: But not if any second, third, etc. people occupy it.]
cards.tag_note: Use the Urgent Wish for Children space even if occupied by another player's first person only.

cards.name: Handplow
cards.text: Add 5 to the current round and place 1 field tile on the corresponding round space. At the start of that round, you can plow the field.
cards.tag_note: Places a field tile on a future round space; at start of that round may plow it.

cards.name: Pond Hut
cards.text: Place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: On play, place 1 food on each of next 3 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.

cards.name: Fire Protection Pond
cards.text: Once you no longer live in a wooden house, place 1 food on each of the next 6 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: House-material latch: on leaving a wooden house, schedule 1 food x6 rounds.

cards.name: Claw Knife
cards.text: Each time you use the "Sheep Market" accumulation space, place 1 food on each of the next 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: Each use of Sheep Market places 1 food on next 2 round spaces.

cards.name: Trellises
cards.text: Immediately place 1 food on each of the next round spaces, up to the number of fences you have built. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: Immediately schedule 1 food on next N round spaces = number of fences built; plain read of fence count.

cards.name: Large Greenhouse
cards.text: Add 4, 7, and 9 to the current round and place 1 vegetable on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the vegetable.
cards.tag_note: On play, place 1 vegetable on round spaces 4/7/9 ahead, collected at start of those rounds.

cards.name: Interim Storage
cards.text: Each time you use a clay/reed/stone accumulation space, place 1 wood/clay/reed on this card. At the start of rounds 7, 11, and 14, move all the goods on this card to your supply.
cards.tag_note: The good placed is fixed by which accumulation space was used — no choice, so F-AUTO not F-MANDCHOICE; the fixed-round (7/11/14) release is scheduled collection (E-SCHED) off the on-card pile (ST-STACK), not an immediate E-GOODS or a separate S-SOR hook.

cards.name: Grassland Harrow
cards.prereq: 2 Occupations, 1 Building Resource in Your Supply
cards.text: Add 1 to the current round for each building resource in your supply and place 1 field on the corresponding round space. A the start of the round, you can plow the field.
cards.tag_note: Removed L-GEOMBOARD: placing a field on a future round space is scheduling (E-SCHED), not action-board position/adjacency geometry.

cards.name: Chain Float
cards.text: Add 7, 8, and 9 to the current round and place 1 field on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you can plow the field.
cards.tag_note: Removed L-GEOMBOARD: placing fields on specific future round spaces is scheduling (E-SCHED), not board position/adjacency geometry.

cards.name: Chophouse
cards.text: Each time you use the "Grain/Vegetable Seed" action space, place 1 food on each of the next 3/2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.  [CLARIFICATION: Note the errata for Swagman A129.]
cards.tag_note: "Each time you use" = T-BEFORE per ruling (labelB right on timing). The 2 Wood/2 Clay cost forks with the 3/2 reward (pay wood -> 3 round spaces, clay -> 2), so it is E-PLAYVARIANT, and the chosen variant must persist to parameterize every later firing (ST-STORE) — both labelers missed this.

cards.name: Strawberry Patch
cards.text: Place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: Place food on next 3 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.

cards.name: Herring Pot
cards.text: Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: Each use of named Fishing space places food on next 3 round spaces (scheduled).

cards.name: Brewing Water
cards.text: Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you can pay 1 grain to place 1 food on each of the next 6 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: Cost is 1 GRAIN, an ordinary resource cost — E-FOODCOST is only for costs paid in food; the effect schedules food on round spaces (E-SCHED). B is correct.

cards.name: Sack Cart
cards.text: Place 1 grain each on the remaining spaces for rounds 5, 8, 11, and 14. At the start of these rounds, you get the grain.
cards.tag_note: Places grain on rounds 5/8/11/14, collected at start of those rounds.

cards.name: Thick Forest
cards.text: Place 1 wood on each remaining even-numbered round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood.
cards.tag_note: Places wood on each remaining even round space, collected at round start.

cards.name: Reed Belt
cards.text: Place 1 reed on each of the remaining space for rounds 5, 8, 10, and 12. At the start of these rounds, you get the reed.
cards.tag_note: On play, place reed on the remaining round-5/8/10/12 spaces.

cards.name: Acorns Basket
cards.text: Place 1 wild boar on each of the 2 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the wild boar.
cards.tag_note: Places wild boar on 2 round spaces, collected at round start; animals must be accommodated.

cards.name: Farm Building
cards.text: Each time you build a major improvement, place 1 food on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: Each time you build a major improvement, place 1 food on each of next 3 round spaces (mandatory schedule).

cards.name: Stew
cards.text: Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, also place 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: Each use of Day Laborer also places 1 food on each of next 4 round spaces.

cards.name: Garden Claw
cards.text: Place 1 food on each remaining round space, up to three times the number of planted fields you have. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: Removed L-GEOMBOARD: placing food on future round spaces is E-SCHED, not board position/order/adjacency geometry.

cards.name: Private Forest
cards.text: Place 1 wood on each remaining even-numbered round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood.
cards.tag_note: Wood on remaining even round spaces, collected at start.

cards.name: Clay Supply
cards.text: Place 1 clay on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the clay.
cards.tag_note: Places 1 clay on next 3 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.

cards.name: Reed-Hatted Toad
cards.text: Add 5, 7, 9, 11, and 13 to the current round and place 1 reed on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the reed.  [CLARIFICATION: Name was Toad in the Wizkids printing.]
cards.tag_note: Places 1 reed on 5 future round spaces (current+5,7,9,11,13), collected at their start.

cards.name: Stone Cart
cards.text: Place 1 stone on each remaining even-numbered round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the stone.
cards.tag_note: Stone on remaining even round spaces, collected at start.

cards.name: Cesspit
cards.text: Alternate placing 1 clay and 1 wild boar on each remaining round space, starting with clay. At the start of these rounds, you get the respective good.
cards.tag_note: Removed L-GEOMBOARD: placing goods/animals on future round spaces is E-SCHED/E-SCHEDANIMAL, not board position/order/adjacency.

cards.name: Horse-Drawn Boat
cards.text: Alternate placing 1 food and 1 sheep on each remaining round space, starting with food. At the start of these rounds, you get the respective good.
cards.tag_note: On play, alternate placing 1 food and 1 sheep on each remaining round space, collected at start of those rounds.

cards.name: Hutch
cards.text: Place 0, 1, 2, and 3 food in this order on the next 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: On play, place 0/1/2/3 food on next 4 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.

cards.name: Forest Well
cards.text: Place 1 food on each remaining round space, up to the amount of wood in your supply. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: Place 1 food on each remaining round space up to your wood count, collected at those round starts.

cards.name: Sheep Well
cards.text: Place 1 food on each of the next round spaces, up to the number of sheep you have. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: On play, place 1 food on each next round space up to sheep count, collected at start of those rounds.

cards.name: Churchyard
cards.text: Place 2 food on each remaining round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the food. (*Occupations and Improvements)  [CLARIFICATION: Cards must be Occupations and Improvements.]
cards.tag_note: Place 2 food on each remaining round space, collected at those round starts.

cards.name: Wholesale Market
cards.text: Place 1 food on each remaining round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: Place 1 food on each remaining round space, collected at start of those rounds.

cards.name: Reap Hook
cards.text: Place 1 grain on each of the next 3 of the round spaces 4, 7, 9, 11, 13, and 14. At the start of these rounds, you get the grain.
cards.tag_note: Places grain on the next 3 harvest round spaces; scheduling onto future round spaces is E-SCHED, not L-HIDDEN (round spaces 4/7/9/... are fixed, not hidden-order).

cards.name: Small Greenhouse
cards.text: Add 4 and 7 to the current round and place 1 vegetable on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you can buy the vegetable for 1 food.
cards.tag_note: ONPLAY schedules vegetables onto two future round spaces (E-SCHED). Collection is NOT automatic — it is an optional buy at the start of those specific rounds, a fixed-timing event, so HOOK S-SOR/F-TRIG/A-OWN (not label A's ATWILL+CAP-ROUND: the player doesn't choose the moment). The buy exchanges 1 food for the veg (E-CONVERT) and costs food (E-FOODCOST).

cards.name: Reed Pond
cards.text: Place 1 reed on each of the next 3 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the reed.
cards.tag_note: Pure scheduling card: place reed on the next 3 round spaces, collected automatically at the start of those rounds — exactly E-SCHED's definition. E-GOODS (label B) is wrong: nothing is gained immediately; the future collection is inherent to E-SCHED.

cards.name: Wall Builder
cards.text: Each time you build at least 1 room, you can place 1 food on each of the next 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.
cards.tag_note: Each time you build >=1 room, may place 1 food on each of next 4 round spaces, collected at those rounds' start.

cards.name: Clay Hut Builder
cards.text: Once you no longer live in a wooden house, place 2 clay on each of the next 5 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the clay.
cards.tag_note: One-shot fires the first moment you no longer live in a wooden house (standing house-material condition) -> LATCH + ST-LATCH + CAP-GAME; places clay on future round spaces -> E-SCHED.

cards.name: Stable Planner
cards.text: Add 3, 6, and 9 to the current round. You can place 1 stable on each corresponding round space. A the start of these rounds (not earlier), you can build the stable at no cost.  [CLARIFICATION: Stables built this way are not built on your turn and do not trigger Stable Tree A074 or Farmyard Manure A043.]
cards.tag_note: On play, stables are scheduled onto future round spaces (E-SCHED — the round spaces, not the card, hold them, so no ST-STORE) and each grants an optional free stable build at the start of that round (S-SOR + F-TRIG + E-GRANTSUB, T-BEFORE by analogy with other start-of-phase hooks); the stables are reserved to be built free, not paid away, so no E-PIECECOST.

cards.name: Manservant
cards.text: Once you live in a stone house, place 3 food on each remaining round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the food.  [CLARIFICATION: If played by the Scholar B097 at the start of Round X, food is placed on Rounds (X+1) to 14.]
cards.tag_note: One-shot standing-condition latch (first live in stone house) that schedules food on remaining round spaces; LATCH pairs with ST-LATCH and CAP-GAME (fires once ever), matching the sibling leave-wooden-house schedule cards.

cards.name: Lumberjack
cards.text: You immediately get 1 wood. Additionally, place 1 wood on each of the next round spaces, up to the number of fences you built. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood.
cards.tag_note: Immediate +1 wood; places wood on next round spaces up to fences built, collected at round start.

cards.name: Estate Worker
cards.text: Place 1 wood, 1 clay, 1 reed, and 1 stone in this order on the next 4 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the respective building resource.
cards.tag_note: Places wood/clay/reed/stone on next 4 round spaces, collected at round start.

cards.name: Tree Farm Joiner
cards.text: Place 1 wood on each of the next 2 odd-numbered round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood and, immediately afterward, a "Minor Improvement" action.
cards.tag_note: On-play schedules wood on future odd round spaces (E-SCHED, not L-GEOMBOARD — scheduling onto future rounds is E-SCHED); at start you gain the wood (E-GOODS) then a Minor Improvement action, which is optional (F-TRIG). Odd-numbered is a scheduling selection, not board adjacency.

cards.name: Wood Collector
cards.text: Place 1 wood on each of the next 5 round spaces. At the start of these rounds, you get the wood.
cards.tag_note: Place 1 wood on each of next 5 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.

cards.name: Cattle Whisperer
cards.text: Add 5 and 8 to the current round and place 1 cattle on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you get the cattle.
cards.tag_note: Place cattle on round spaces +5/+8, collected at start of those rounds.

cards.name: Plowman
cards.text: Add 4, 7, and 10 to the current round and place a field tile on each corresponding round space. At the start of these rounds, you can plow the field for 1 food.
cards.tag_note: Plowman: schedule field tiles onto rounds +4/+7/+10 (E-SCHED); at the START of those rounds optionally plow for 1 food -> T-BEFORE (user ruling).

cards.name: Carpenter's Axe
cards.text: Each time after you use a wood accumulation space, if you then have at least 7 wood in your supply, you can build exactly 1 stable for 1 wood.
cards.tag_note: Optional stable build after using a wood accumulation space; the 1-wood price is an ordinary resource cost on the granted sub-action (not E-PIECECOST — that is for paying WITH a stable/fence piece), and the 7-wood condition is a plain state read after the space resolves, needing no event provenance (no ST-PROV).

cards.name: Wheel Plow
cards.text: Once this game, when you use the "Farmland" or Cultivation" action space with the first person you place in a round, you can plow 2 additional fields.
cards.tag_note: 'When you use Farmland/Cultivation' → T-BEFORE per the default ruling; 'first person you place in a round' is computable from state (your placed-worker count), so no ST-PLACELOG, and CAP-GAME covers the once-per-game flag without ST-STORE.

cards.name: Threshing Board
cards.text: Each time you use the "Farmland" or "Cultivation" action space, you get an additional "Bake Bread" action.
cards.tag_note: "Each time you use [space]" fires T-BEFORE by explicit ruling (T-AFTER only for 'immediately after'/'at the end of'); the granted Bake Bread sub-action is optional (F-TRIG).

cards.name: Forest Lake Hut
cards.text: Each time you use the "Fishing"/"Forest" accumulation space, you also get 1 wood/food.
cards.tag_note: Each time you use Fishing/Forest space you also get 1 wood/food; automatic per-use.

cards.name: Milk Jug
cards.text: Each time any player (including you) uses the "Cattle Market" accumulation space, you get 3 food, and each other player gets 1 food.
cards.tag_note: Each time any player uses Cattle Market: you get 3 food, each other player gets 1 food (transfer/gain to others).

cards.name: Drift-Net Boat
cards.text: Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you get an additional 2 food.
cards.tag_note: Each use of Fishing space grants an additional 2 food.

cards.name: Throwing Axe
cards.text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space while there is at least 1 wild boar on the "Pig Market" accumulation space, you also get 2 food.
cards.tag_note: Each use of a wood accumulation space, if boar present on Pig Market, also get 2 food; conditional board-state read.

cards.name: Basket
cards.text: Immediately after each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can exchange 2 wood for 3 food. If you do, place those 2 wood on the accumulation space.  [CLARIFICATION: You may take less than 2 wood from a wood accumulation space and still use this effect.  Food obtained from this effect may not be used to pay for Shifting Cultivator A091.]
cards.tag_note: Optional post-space exchange 2 wood → 3 food is a pure conversion (E-CONVERT); tagging the food proceeds as E-GOODS double-counts the conversion. The returned wood goes onto the space, not the player's supply.

cards.name: Feeding Dish
cards.text: Each time you use an animal accumulation space while already having an animal of that type, you get 1 grain.  [CLARIFICATION: Animal Dealer A147‘s effect can be used before this.]
cards.tag_note: 'Each time you use [space]' fires T-BEFORE by ruling (the already-have-that-animal condition is checked before taking); mandatory grain gain on an own-space use — labelA only missed the mandatory A-OWN.

cards.name: Corn Scoop
cards.text: Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, you get 1 additional grain.
cards.tag_note: Each time you use Grain Seeds space, get 1 additional grain.

cards.name: Calcium Fertilizers
cards.text: Each time you use a "Quarry" accumulation space, add 1 additional good of the respective type to each of your planted fields growing a single type of crop.
cards.tag_note: 'Each time you use a Quarry space' → T-BEFORE per the explicit ruling (labelA's T-AFTER contradicts it), A-OWN mandatory; adding goods onto planted fields is crop-on-field manipulation.

cards.name: Hod
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 clay. Each time any player (including you) uses the "Pig Market" accumulation space, you immediately get 2 clay.
cards.tag_note: Fires on ANY player's Pig Market use → tag both A-OWN and A-OPP; 'each time ... uses' defaults to T-BEFORE ('immediately get' is not 'immediately after'); plus on-play +1 clay.

cards.name: Canoe
cards.text: Each time you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you get an additional 1 food and 1 reed.
cards.tag_note: Each time you use Fishing space, get 1 additional food and 1 reed.

cards.name: Stone Tongs
cards.text: Each time you use a stone accumulation space, you get 1 additional stone.
cards.tag_note: Each time you use a stone accumulation space, get 1 additional stone.

cards.name: Moldboard Plow
cards.text: Place 2 field tiles on this card. Twice this game, when you use the "Farmland" action space, you can also plow 1 field from this card.
cards.tag_note: 'When you use the Farmland action space' is the 'each time you use [space]' shape -> T-BEFORE per the ruling; optional extra plow = E-GRANTSUB/F-TRIG, twice per game = CAP-GAME. The 2 field tiles on the card are a consumed pool (ST-STACK) that itself tracks uses, so ST-STORE is redundant.

cards.name: Brewery Pond
cards.text: Each time you use the "Fishing" or "Reed Bank" accumulation space, you also get 1 grain and 1 wood.
cards.tag_note: "Each time you use [space]" fires BEFORE the space's action per the explicit ruling; labelB's T-BEFORE is correct, all other tags agree.

cards.name: Digging Spade
cards.text: Each time you use a clay accumulation space, you also get a number of food equal to the number of wild boar in your farmyard.
cards.tag_note: Each clay-space use grants food equal to wild boar count.

cards.name: Maintenance Premium
cards.text: Place 3 food on this card. Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you get 1 food from this card. Each time you renovate restock this card to 3 food.
cards.tag_note: Two hooks: wood accumulation space use (S-SPACE) AND 'each time you renovate' restock — renovate is a primitive sub-action, so S-SUB is required; B is correct to include it.

cards.name: Pitchfork
cards.text: Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, if the "Farmland" action space is occupied you also get 3 food.
cards.tag_note: Each time you use Grain Seeds, if Farmland occupied, get 3 food; conditional on a named space's occupancy.

cards.name: Tasting
cards.text: Each time you use a "Lessons" action space, before paying the occupation cost, you can exchange 1 grain for 4 food.
cards.tag_note: Optional grain→food exchange on using Lessons; the food is proceeds of the conversion, so E-CONVERT alone — adding E-GOODS double-counts the same effect.

cards.name: Mill Wheel
cards.text: Each time you use the "Grain Utilization" action space while the "Fishing" accumulation space is occupied, you get an additional 2 food.
cards.tag_note: Grain Utilization use while Fishing occupied grants +2 food; needs board occupancy read.

cards.name: Loam Pit
cards.text: Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you also get 3 clay.  [CLARIFICATION: These clay may be spent to pay for the effect of the Cottager B087.]
cards.tag_note: Each time you use Day Laborer, also get 3 clay (spendable for Cottager).

cards.name: Corf
cards.text: Each time any player (including you) takes at least 3 stone from an accumulation space, you get 1 stone from the general supply.
cards.tag_note: Trigger is source-specific — taking stone FROM an accumulation space (an action-space use), not obtaining stone from any source — so S-SPACE, not S-OBTAIN; the ≥3-stone check needs the take's payload (ST-PROV), fires after the take on any player's action (A-OWN+A-OPP), mandatory gain (F-AUTO, E-GOODS).

cards.name: Trellis
cards.text: Each time before you use the "Pig Market" accumulation space, you can take a "Build Fences" action. (You must pay wood for the fences as usual.)
cards.tag_note: Each time before using Pig Market space, may take a full Build Fences action (pay wood as usual) -> T-BEFORE, grants a whole action.

cards.name: Swing Plow
cards.text: Place 4 field tiles on this card. Each time you use the "Farmland" action space, you can also plow up to 2 fields from this card.
cards.tag_note: On play 4 field tiles are piled on the card (ONPLAY + ST-STACK — the taxonomy explicitly says a pool of field tiles plowed ONTO the farm is ST-STACK, NOT L-CARDFIELD); "each time you use Farmland" is an own-action space hook (T-BEFORE default) granting optional extra plows (F-TRIG + E-GRANTSUB).

cards.name: Mole Plow
cards.text: Each time you use the "Farmland" or "Cultivation" action space, you can plow 1 additional field.
cards.tag_note: Each time using Farmland/Cultivation space, may plow 1 additional field -> T-BEFORE grant of plow sub-action.

cards.name: Steam Machine
cards.text: Each work phase, if the last action space you use is an accumulation space, you can immediately afterward take a "Bake Bread" action.
cards.tag_note: Bake Bread is a primitive sub-action per the taxonomy (E-GRANTSUB explicitly lists bake bread), so E-GRANTSUB not E-GRANTACT; 'immediately afterward' = T-AFTER on the space use. ST-PLACELOG is unneeded: the hook fires at the space use itself, and 'last' is computable from workers remaining — no persistent per-worker log.

cards.name: Flail
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 2 food. Each time you use the "Farmland" or "Cultivation" action space, you can also take a "Bake Bread" action.
cards.tag_note: 'Take a Bake Bread action' grants the bake-bread primitive sub-action → E-GRANTSUB (same call as C25); 'each time you use' = T-BEFORE default; on-play 2 food = ONPLAY+E-GOODS. Otherwise the labels agree.

cards.name: Teacher's Desk
cards.text: Each time you use the "Major Improvement" or "House Redevelopment" action space, you can also play 1 occupation at an occupation cost of 1 food.
cards.tag_note: 'Each time you use [space]' = HOOK T-BEFORE S-SPACE, optional grant (F-TRIG); playing a card is listed as a primitive sub-action (S-SUB seam), so granting 'play 1 occupation' is E-GRANTSUB, not a whole-space E-GRANTACT; the 1-food occupation cost is E-FOODCOST. No L-EXT needed -- the grant itself is the legality.

cards.name: Clay Deposit
cards.text: Immediately after each time you use a clay accumulation space, you can exchange 1 clay for 1 bonus point. If you do, place the clay on the accumulation space.
cards.tag_note: Optional after-hook on using a clay accumulation space. Clay->bonus point is not E-CONVERT (bonus point isn't a good); the clay is placed on the ACCUMULATION space, not a future round space, so E-SCHED is wrong — placing a good back onto an action space fits no code, hence EXOTIC. Earned bonus points need ST-COUNTER.

cards.name: Woodcraft
cards.text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space, if immediately afterward you have at most 5 wood in your supply, you get 1 food.  [CLARIFICATION: This effect is checked before cards that trigger “after”, e.g. Tree Guard C102.]
cards.tag_note: Auto after-hook on wood accumulation spaces; the <=5-wood condition is a plain read of current supply at fire time, so no ST-PROV needed (the Tree Guard clarification is firing ORDER, not stored state).

cards.name: Wood Cart
cards.text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you get 2 additional wood.
cards.tag_note: Each time using a wood accumulation space, +2 wood. Space-seam hook, own, automatic.

cards.name: Hardware Store
cards.text: Each time after you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you can pay 2 food total to buy 1 wood, 1 clay, 1 reed, and 1 stone.
cards.tag_note: Optional after-Day-Laborer purchase: 2 food exchanged for four resources — a food-cost conversion yielding goods (E-CONVERT + E-FOODCOST + E-GOODS), same shape as C80's buy.

cards.name: Pulverizer Plow
cards.text: Immediately after each time you use a clay accumulation space, you can pay 1 clay to plow 1 field. If you do, place that 1 clay on the accumulation space.
cards.tag_note: After-space hook granting an optional plow; placing 'that 1 clay' back on THE triggering accumulation space needs the event's payload (which space fired) -> ST-PROV.

cards.name: Turnwrest Plow
cards.text: Place 2 field tiles on this card. Each time you use the "Farmland" or "Cultivation" action space, you can also plow up to 2 fields from this card.
cards.tag_note: Card holds a POOL of 2 field tiles you plow onto your farm (ST-STACK); the card is not itself a sown/harvested field -> L-CARDFIELD removed.

cards.name: Writing Desk
cards.text: Each time you use a "Lessons" action space, you can play 1 additional occupation for an occupation cost of 2 food.
cards.tag_note: Each time you use a Lessons space you may play 1 additional occupation for 2 food. Playing a card is a primitive sub-action = E-GRANTSUB; the 2-food occupation cost is E-FOODCOST.

cards.name: Truffle Slicer
cards.text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space, if you have at least 1 wild boar, you can pay 1 food for 1 bonus point.
cards.tag_note: Optional pay-1-food-for-1-bonus-point on each wood-accumulation-space use — the key ruling explicitly sanctions HOOK + E-SCORE for bonus points earned through an action, so labelA's EXOTIC is wrong; earned bonus points accumulate over the game (ST-COUNTER); 'each time you use' → T-BEFORE.

cards.name: New Market
cards.text: Each time you use an action space card on round spaces 8 to 11, you get 1 additional food.
cards.tag_note: 'Each time you use [a space]' fires T-BEFORE by explicit ruling; round spaces 8-11 is a fixed band, not board geometry — labelB correct.

cards.name: Small Basket
cards.text: Each time you use the "Reed Bank" accumulation space, you can pay 1 reed to get 1 vegetable. If you do in a game with 4+ players, place that 1 reed on the accumulation space.
cards.tag_note: Each use of Reed Bank you may pay 1 reed for 1 veg; 4p reed-placement branch irrelevant to 2p.

cards.name: Supply Boat
cards.text: Each time after you use the "Fishing" accumulation space, you can choose to buy 1 grain for 1 food, or 1 vegetable for 3 food.
cards.tag_note: Optional buy each time after using Fishing (S-SPACE/T-AFTER/F-TRIG/A-OWN). The buy is a food-for-crop exchange at a rate (E-CONVERT) whose price is food, payable by converting (E-FOODCOST — label A missed it). E-GOODS (label B) is redundant: the gained crop is the conversion's output, not an independent grant.

cards.name: Ox Goad
cards.text: Each time after you use the "Cattle Market" accumulation space, you can pay 2 food to plow 1 field.
cards.tag_note: After using Cattle Market, may pay 2 food to plow 1 field.

cards.name: Portmonger
cards.text: Each time you take 1/2/3+ food from a food accumulation space, you also get 1 vegetable/grain/reed.
cards.tag_note: After taking food from a food accumulation space you also gain a good (E-GOODS, F-AUTO, T-AFTER); the reward depends on the amount of food taken, needing event payload provenance (ST-PROV).

cards.name: Catcher
cards.text: Each time you place your 1st/2nd/3rd person in a round on a building resource accumulation space with exactly 5/4/3 building resources, you get 1 food.
cards.tag_note: Fires on placing on a space and must read the resource count before the space's action collects it → T-BEFORE; the 1st/2nd/3rd-placement ordinal is derivable from the board (your placed workers this round), so no ST-COUNTER/ST-PLACELOG is required.

cards.name: Mushroom Collector
cards.text: Immediately after each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can exchange 1 wood for 2 food. If you do, place the wood on the accumulation space.  [CLARIFICATION: You may take less than 1 wood from a wood accumulation space and still use this effect.  Food obtained from this effect may not be used to pay for Shifting Cultivator A091.]
cards.tag_note: 'Immediately after each time you use a wood accumulation space' = HOOK/T-AFTER/S-SPACE, optional ('you can') = F-TRIG, own action. The 1-wood-for-2-food swap is E-CONVERT (the food it yields is the conversion output, not a separate E-GOODS). ST-PROV kept: the effect must place the wood back on THE specific accumulation space just used — it consumes the trigger event's payload (which space fired).

cards.name: Heresy Teacher
cards.text: Each time you use a "Lessons" action space, you get 1 vegetable in each of your fields with at least 3 grain and no vegetable. Place the vegetable below the grain.  [CLARIFICATION: Fields with both crops can count as a grain field or a vegetable field, but not both simultaneously.]
cards.tag_note: 'Each time you use Lessons' (T-BEFORE space hook, F-AUTO) places a vegetable onto qualifying existing fields (crops-on-fields = E-CROPMANIP, not loose E-GOODS); uses regular fields so not L-CARDFIELD.

cards.name: Seasonal Worker
cards.text: Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you get 1 additional grain. From round 6 on, you can choose to get 1 vegetable instead.
cards.tag_note: 'Each time you use Day Laborer' = HOOK/S-SPACE/T-BEFORE (default), own action, mandatory gain (E-GOODS). Before round 6 it is choice-free F-AUTO; from round 6 on it is mandatory-with-a-choice (grain OR vegetable, no decline) = F-MANDCHOICE — both firing regimes exist over the game, so both tags apply (labelB).

cards.name: Wood Cutter
cards.text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you get 1 additional wood.
cards.tag_note: Each use of a wood accumulation space -> +1 wood, mandatory.

cards.name: Clay Puncher
cards.text: When you play this card and each time after you use a "Lessons" action space or the "Clay Pit" accumulation space, you get 1 clay.  [CLARIFICATION: Gives 1+1=2 clay when played on Lessons.]
cards.tag_note: On play get 1 clay, and each time after using Lessons or Clay Pit get 1 clay.

cards.name: Pan Baker
cards.text: Each time you use the "Grain Utilization" action space, you also get 2 clay and 1 wood.
cards.tag_note: Each time you use Grain Utilization, also get 2 clay and 1 wood.

cards.name: Shifting Cultivator
cards.text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you can also play 3 food to plow 1 field.  [CLARIFICATION: Food obtained via the Basket A056, or any other effect “after” using the space may not be used to pay for this effect.]
cards.tag_note: 'Each time you use' fires T-BEFORE, and the clarification (after-effect food can't pay) is just a consequence of that ordering — payment is checked against current supply at fire time, no snapshot/payload needed, so drop ST-PROV; optional 3-food plow grant (F-TRIG, E-FOODCOST, E-GRANTSUB).

cards.name: Oven Firing Boy
cards.text: Each time you use a wood accumulation space, you get an additional "Bake Bread" action.
cards.tag_note: 'Each time you use a wood accumulation space' = own-turn hook, T-BEFORE by ruling; grants optional Bake Bread sub-action. It grants the standard bake sub-action, not a new baking rate, so no E-BAKESPEC.

cards.name: Geologist
cards.text: Each time you use the "Forest" or "Reed Bank" accumulation space, you also get 1 clay. In games with 3 or more players, this also applies to the "Clay Pit".
cards.tag_note: Each time you use Forest/Reed Bank space, also get 1 clay (before, auto).

cards.name: Mineralogist
cards.text: Each time you use a clay/stone accumulation space, you also get 1 of the other good, stone/clay.
cards.tag_note: 'Each time you use [space]' takes the T-BEFORE default per the explicit ruling — only 'immediately after'/'at the end of' wording earns T-AFTER; mandatory extra good on using a clay/stone accumulation space.

cards.name: Cottager
cards.text: Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you can also either build exactly 1 room or renovate your house. Either way, you have to pay the cost.
cards.tag_note: Standard optional space rider: 'each time you use Day Laborer' (T-BEFORE default), 'you can also' (F-TRIG), granting build-1-room or renovate at normal cost (E-GRANTSUB). Nothing in the text touches occupied-space legality, so labelA's L-OCCUPY is spurious.

cards.name: Cooperative Plower
cards.text: Each time you use the "Farmland" action space while the "Grain Seeds" action space is occupied, you can plow 1 additional field.
cards.tag_note: Removed L-GEOMBOARD: only reads occupancy of the named 'Grain Seeds' space, not board position/order/adjacency.

cards.name: Assistant Tiller
cards.text: Each time you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you can also plow 1 field.
cards.tag_note: Own-turn hook on using Day Laborer space, granting optional plow before the action; A-OWN required, no L-OCCUPY (nothing about occupied spaces).

cards.name: Tree Guard
cards.text: Each time after you use a wood accumulation space, you can place 4 wood from your supply on that space to get 2 stone, 1 clay, 1 reed, and 1 grain.
cards.tag_note: After using a wood accumulation space, pay 4 wood onto it to get 2 stone/1 clay/1 reed/1 grain. After-timing on named-space use; pays wood for goods.

cards.name: Thresher
cards.text: Immediately before each time you use the "Grain Utilization", "Farmland", or "Cultivation" action space, you can buy 1 grain for 1 food.  [CLARIFICATION: This effect happens before using the space, and must happen before effects such as Flail C026.]
cards.tag_note: Before-use space hook (clarification confirms T-BEFORE), optional buy of 1 grain for 1 food = E-CONVERT plus E-FOODCOST (effect costs food, payable via liquidation — matches buy-for-food precedents).

cards.name: Soil Scientist
cards.text: Each time after you use a clay/stone accumulation space, you can place 1 stone/2 clay from your supply on the space to get 2 grain/1 vegetable, respectively.
cards.tag_note: After using a clay/stone accumulation space, pay stone/clay onto the space to get grain/veg. After-timing named-space hook; resource conversion.

cards.name: Clay Kneader
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood and 2 clay. Each time after you use a "Grain Seeds" or "Vegetable Seeds" action space, you get 1 clay.
cards.tag_note: On play +1 wood +2 clay; after using Grain Seeds or Vegetable Seeds space, +1 clay.

cards.name: Excavator
cards.text: Each time after you use the "Day Laborer" action space, you get 1 additional wood and clay, and you can buy 1 stone for 1 food.  [CLARIFICATION: These resources may not be used to pay for the effect of the Cottager B087.]
cards.tag_note: After-Day-Laborer hook with two parts: mandatory +1 wood +1 clay (F-AUTO, E-GOODS) and an optional buy of 1 stone for 1 food (F-TRIG, E-CONVERT, E-FOODCOST) — both firing kinds genuinely apply.

cards.name: Private Teacher
cards.text: Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space when any "Lessons" action space is occupied, you can also play an occupation for an occupation cost of 1 food.  [CLARIFICATION: If the played occupation has an effect when using “Grain Seeds”, it also triggers immediately.]
cards.tag_note: Optional grant of the play-an-occupation primitive (play-a-card is listed as a primitive sub-action -> E-GRANTSUB over E-GRANTACT) at a 1-food occupation cost (E-FOODCOST); Lessons occupancy is a plain named-space state read, no geometry code.

cards.name: Sheep Provider
cards.text: Each time any player (including you) uses the "Sheep Market" accumulation space, you get 1 grain.
cards.tag_note: Any player uses Sheep Market -> you get 1 grain (fires on own and opponent use).

cards.name: Market Crier
cards.text: Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, you can get an additional 1 grain and 1 vegetable. If you do, each other player gets 1 grain from the general supply.
cards.tag_note: 'Each time you use [space]' fires T-BEFORE per the categorical ruling (no 'after' wording); optional extra grain+veg (F-TRIG, E-GOODS) with a linked grain gift to each other player (E-OPPTRANSFER).

cards.name: Cowherd
cards.text: Each time you use the "Cattle Market" accumulation space (introduced in round 10 or 11), you get 1 additional cattle.
cards.tag_note: "Each time you use [space]" fires T-BEFORE per the explicit ruling; "you get" = F-AUTO; the extra cattle is E-ANIMALS (an animal, not a good, so no E-GOODS).

cards.name: Field Watchman
cards.text: Each time you use the "Grain Seeds" action space, you can also plow 1 field.
cards.tag_note: Each time you use Grain Seeds space, optionally also plow 1 field (before, granted sub-action).

cards.name: Plow Hero
cards.text: Each time you use the "Farmland" or "Cultivation" action space with the first person you place in a round, you can plow 1 additional field for 1 food.
cards.tag_note: Use-space hook (T-BEFORE default), optional granted plow (F-TRIG + E-GRANTSUB) for food (E-FOODCOST). No ST-PLACELOG: 'first person you place in a round' is checkable at fire time from your placed-worker count (==1), and ST-PLACELOG is for knowing which SPACE a worker sits on — here the space is the trigger itself.

cards.name: Plow Maker
cards.text: Each time you use the "Farmland" or "Cultivation" action space, you can pay 1 food to plow 1 additional field.
cards.tag_note: On Farmland/Cultivation, pay 1 food to plow an additional field.

cards.name: Nest Site
cards.text: Each time 1 reed is placed on a non-empty "Reed Bank" accumulation space during the preparation phase, you get 1 food.
cards.tag_note: The trigger is the preparation-phase accumulation refill of the Reed Bank (a start-of-round system event), not a card reveal — S-SOR, not S-REVEAL; food gain is automatic after the reed lands on a non-empty space.

cards.name: Cob
cards.text: At the start of each work phase, if you have at least 1 clay in your supply, you can exchange exactly 1 grain for 2 clay and 1 food.
cards.tag_note: Optional grain-for-clay+food exchange at the start of each work phase (before work begins → T-BEFORE); a pure exchange is E-CONVERT, so E-GOODS is redundant.

cards.name: Scullery
cards.text: At the start of each round, if you live in a wooden house, you get 1 food.
cards.tag_note: Plain 'at the start of each round' hook fires before the round's work — matches the adjudicated convention for identical wording (B97/E100/E102 all T-BEFORE); wooden-house check is a plain state read, mandatory gain = F-AUTO + E-GOODS.

cards.name: Civic Facade
cards.text: Before the start of each round, if you have more occupations than improvements in your hand, you get 1 food.
cards.tag_note: Before start of each round, conditional on hand composition (occupations > improvements in hand) get 1 food.

cards.name: Trout Pool
cards.text: At the start of each work phase, if there are at least 3 food on the "Fishing" accumulation space, you get 1 food from the general supply.
cards.tag_note: At start of each work phase, if >=3 food on Fishing space, get 1 food; reads a specific named accumulation space (not L-GEOMBOARD).

cards.name: Plow Driver
cards.text: Once you live in a stone house, at the start of each round, you can pay 1 food to plow 1 field.  [CLARIFICATION: If played by the Scholar B097, the effect can be used immediately.]
cards.tag_note: Recurring optional start-of-round plow-for-food, gated on a standing stone-house state read. Not LATCH/ST-LATCH (LATCH is a ONE-time fire; this repeats every round once true), and no CAP-ROUND (an 'each round' hook implies once-per-occurrence by nature). T-AFTER: the effect fires at/within the start-of-round seam, matching the phase-seam convention (cf. field-phase hooks); no 'before' wording.

cards.name: Pavior
cards.text: At the end of each preparation phase, if you have at least 1 stone in your supply, you get 1 food. In round 14, you get 1 vegetable instead.
cards.tag_note: The preparation phase IS the start-of-round phase, so 'at the end of each preparation phase' is S-SOR (+T-AFTER for the 'end of' wording), not S-ROUNDEND (returning home / end of round); mandatory goods on a plain state read.

cards.name: Childless
cards.text: At the start of each round, if you have at least 3 rooms but only 2 people, you get 1 food and 1 crop of your choice (grain or vegetable)  [CLARIFICATION: Cards that provide room for a person do not count for this effect unless they self-identify as a room.]
cards.tag_note: 'At the start of each round' resolves at the SOR seam = T-AFTER by convention (T-BEFORE is reserved for 'before the start of each round'); mandatory gain with a grain-or-vegetable choice = F-MANDCHOICE.

cards.name: Small-scale Farmer
cards.text: As long as you live in a house with exactly 2 rooms, at the start of each round, you get 1 wood.  [CLARIFICATION: Cards that provide room for a person do not count for this effect unless they self-identify as a room.]
cards.tag_note: Recurring start-of-round auto gain while a plain house-size condition holds (re-checked each round, so a hook, not a latch); 'at the start of each round' = T-AFTER per the SOR convention.

cards.name: Groom
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 wood. Once you live in a stone house, at the start of each round, you can build exactly 1 stable for 1 wood.  [CLARIFICATION: If played by the Scholar B097, the effect can be used immediately.]
cards.tag_note: On-play +1 wood (ONPLAY/E-GOODS). The stable grant is a RECURRING start-of-each-round hook gated on a standing state check (stone house) — recurring means it is NOT a one-shot LATCH/ST-LATCH, just a conditional S-SOR hook. Cost is 1 wood (an ordinary resource), so no E-PIECECOST; the built stable is the effect, not a piece paid.

cards.name: Scholar
cards.text: Once you live in a stone house, at the start of each round, you can play an occupation for an occupation cost of 1 food, or a minor improvement (by paying its cost).  [ERRATA: The version found at Play-Agricola has wrong text!]  [CLARIFICATION: Cards with text such as “remaining rounds” (e.g. Manservant B107) will NOT trigger in time for the current round.]
cards.tag_note: Recurring start-of-EACH-round option once the stone-house condition holds — recurring, so a conditional S-SOR hook, not LATCH/ST-LATCH. Play-a-card is listed as a primitive sub-action in the S-SUB seam, so the grant is E-GRANTSUB; the 1-food occupation cost is the grant's own price (E-FOODCOST), not a modification of a standard build/improvement cost, so no E-COSTMOD.

cards.name: Small Animal Breeder
cards.text: Before the start of each round, if you have food equal to or higher than the current round number (e.g., 8+ food in round 8), you get 1 food.
cards.tag_note: Before start of each round, if food >= round number, get 1 food. Start-of-round auto gain conditional on a plain count read.

cards.name: Freemason
cards.text: As long as you live in a clay/stone house with exactly 2 rooms, at the start of each work phase, you get 2 clay/stone.  [CLARIFICATION: Cards that provide room for a person do not count for this effect unless they self-identify as a room.]
cards.tag_note: 'At the start of each work phase' resolves AT the round-start seam — exact-phrase precedent tags T-AFTER (T-BEFORE is reserved for 'before the start of...'); mandatory conditional goods gated on a plain house-state read.

cards.name: Baking Sheet
cards.text: Each time you take a "Bake Bread" action, you can use this card to exchange exactly 1 grain for 2 food and 1 bonus point.  [CLARIFICATION: You must bake normally to make this exchange.]
cards.tag_note: "Each time you take a Bake Bread action" = T-BEFORE by the default ruling (no 'immediately after' wording); grain→food is a real good-for-good exchange (E-CONVERT), so B's extra E-GOODS is redundant; A-OWN mandatory on own-action hooks; accrued bonus points = ST-COUNTER.

cards.name: Bucksaw
cards.text: Each time you renovate, you can also pay 1 wood to get 1 bonus point and 1 grain.
cards.tag_note: "Each time you renovate" = own renovate sub-action hook, T-BEFORE by the each-time default (A's T-AFTER lacks 'immediately after' wording); wood cost is ordinary (no code); gains 1 grain (E-GOODS) + a bonus point (E-SCORE) that accrues (ST-COUNTER); A-OWN is mandatory on own-action hooks.

cards.name: Junk Room
cards.text: Each time after you build an improvement, including this one, you get 1 food.
cards.tag_note: Each time after you build an improvement (a card being played/built), get 1 food.

cards.name: Dutch Windmill
cards.text: Each time you take a "Bake Bread" action in a round immediately following a harvest, you get 3 additional food.
cards.tag_note: 'Round immediately following a harvest' is round TIMING (fixed every game), not board layout -> L-GEOMBOARD removed.

cards.name: Seed Pellets
cards.text: Each time before you take an unconditional "Sow" action, you get 1 grain.
cards.tag_note: Mandatory grain gain before your own Sow sub-action; labelA merely omitted the required A-OWN default actor.

cards.name: Asparagus Gift
cards.text: Each time you build a number of fences equal to or greater than the current round, you immediately get 1 vegetable.
cards.tag_note: Removed L-GEOMBOARD: 'fences >= current round' is a count-vs-current-round-number threshold, not action-board position/order/adjacency; added A-OWN.

cards.name: Garden Hoe
cards.text: Each time you take an unconditional "Sow" action planting vegetables in at least 1 field, you get 1 clay and 1 stone.
cards.tag_note: 'You get' is mandatory → F-AUTO; the condition (vegetables planted in ≥1 field) requires the sow's payload, so it fires after the sow with ST-PROV.

cards.name: Shepherd's Crook
cards.text: Each time you fence a new pasture covering at least 4 farmyard spaces, you immediately get 2 sheep on this pasture.  [CLARIFICATION: To receive sheep, new pasture(s) can’t be divided.  Building 2 such pastures at once gives you 4 sheep.]
cards.tag_note: Fires after the fence sub-action; '≥4 spaces' is a pasture size COUNT, which the L-GEOMFARM ruling explicitly excludes; the hook needs the just-built pasture's identity/size (undivided check, sheep placed on it) → ST-PROV; mandatory sheep (F-AUTO, E-ANIMALS).

cards.name: Mining Hammer
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food. Each time you renovate, you can also build a stable without paying wood.
cards.tag_note: On-play +1 food; each renovate (a sub-action) optionally grants a free stable build ('can also' -> T-AFTER, F-TRIG). A stable is not a fence (no E-FREEFENCE) and E-PIECECOST is for play-costs paid with a piece, not a free grant.

cards.name: Bread Paddle
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 1 food. For each occupation you play, you get an additional "Bake Bread" action.  [CLARIFICATION: Note the errata for Freshman A097.]
cards.tag_note: On-play +1 food; each occupation played grants an optional extra Bake Bread sub-action (S-PLAY, T-AFTER, F-TRIG). It grants the action, it does not add/change a baking improvement or rate, so no E-BAKESPEC.

cards.name: Tumbrel
cards.text: When you play this card, you immediately get 2 food. Each time after you take an unconditional "Sow" action, you get 1 food for each stable you have.
cards.tag_note: On play +2 food; after unconditional Sow, food per stable.

cards.name: Hand Truck
cards.text: Each time before you take a "Bake Bread" action, you also get 1 grain for each of your people occupying an accumulation space.  [CLARIFICATION: You must bake if you receive the grain.]
cards.tag_note: Grain count = number of YOUR people on accumulation spaces, which requires knowing which spaces your workers occupy — exactly ST-PLACELOG's definition; otherwise both labels agree.

cards.name: Wood Workshop
cards.text: Each time before you play or build an improvement, you get 1 wood.  [CLARIFICATION: You are able to pay for the improvement with just the wood given by this card.]
cards.tag_note: You simply receive 1 wood before playing/building any improvement — the improvement's cost is unchanged (the clarification only confirms the wood arrives in time to pay), so E-COSTMOD is wrong; the trigger covers both minor plays (S-PLAY) and major builds (S-MAJMIN).

cards.name: Beer Stein
cards.text: Each time you take a "Bake Bread" action, you can use this card once to turn 1 grain into 2 food and 1 bonus point.  [CLARIFICATION: You must bake normally to make this exchange.]
